2026-04-20T11:01:37.3416551Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T11:01:37.3417357Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T11:01:37.3418340Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T11:01:37.3418912Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T11:01:37.3419331Z [36;1m[0m

CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). hex\\n- Priority: request payload → env var → deterministic hash → random\\n- Partition hint extracted from conversation_id or previous_response_id\\n- Default agent name 'server-default-agent' when null/empty\\n\\n11 unit tests + 8 protocol tests updated for new format.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* chore: remove Python references InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware that logs all incoming HTTP requests\\nwith method, path, status code, duration, correlation headers\\n(x-request-id, x-ms-client-request-id), and OpenTelemetry trace ID.\\nUses LoggerMessage source generation for high-performance structured\\nlogging. Request start logs at Information level; successful completion\\nat Information; 4xx/5xx responses at Warning. Query strings are\\ndeliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add handler-level diagnostic logging to Responses endpoints\\n\\nAdd Info-level structured logs to all Responses API endpoints:\\n- POST /responses: responseId, streaming, background, store, model,\\n  conversationId, previousResponseId (moved after ID generation)\\n- POST /responses (non-streaming): final status and output count\\n- POST /responses (background): signal handoff with status\\n- GET /responses: responseId, retrieved status and output count\\n- POST /responses/{id}/cancel: responseId and result status\\n- DELETE /responses/{id}: responseId and success\\n- GET /responses/{id}/input_items: responseId\\n- Orchestrator: handler type name on invocation\\n\\nThese logs complement the Core inbound middleware (HTTP-level) with\\nresponse-specific context for debugging storage deserialization issues,\\nhandler failures, and request tracing across the full pipeline.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Update release dates to the pipeline.\\n- ResponseEndpointHandler: cache GetConversationId() result to avoid\\n  repeated JSON parsing across partition key, logging, and session ID.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot endpoint response\\nMUST include x-agent-session-id as a response header.\\n\\nResponses protocol:\\n- Add SessionIdResponseHeaderFilter endpoint filter (outermost, wraps\\n  exception filter) that assertions on existing GET/Cancel tests\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Rename ServerUserAgent to ServerVersion and restructure middleware extensions\\n\\nRename all ServerUserAgent* types, files, methods, and properties to\\nServerVersion* — the header identifies the server version stack, not a\\nuser-agent string.\\n\\nType renames:\\n- ServerUserAgentRegistry → ServerVersionRegistry\\n- ServerUserAgentMiddleware → ServerVersionMiddleware\\n- AgentHostBuilder.UserAgentRegistry → VersionRegistry\\n\\nExtension method restructure:\\n- AddAgentServerUserAgent() → AddAgentServerVersion() (version only)\\n- UseAgentServerUserAgent() → UseAgentServerVersion()\\n- NEW: AddAgentServerLogging() — registers InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware\\n- NEW: UseAgentServerLogging() — wires InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware\\n\\nThe old AddAgentServerUserAgent() bundled both version and logging\\nregistration which was confusing. Now each concern has its own pair of\\nAdd/Use methods. Tier 1/2 (AgentHostBuilder) lights up both\\nautomatically; Tier 3 users call them independently.\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Update changelogs for session ID header and ServerVersion rename\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Add inbound request logging entries to Responses and Invocations changelogs\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Move inbound logging to Features Added in changelogs\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Copilot \\u003c223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com\\u003e\\n\\n* Internalize framework-only telemetry types\\n\\nMake AgentHostTelemetry, InvocationsActivitySource, ResponsesActivitySource,\\nand ResponsesTracingConstants internal.
